name: What information do I actually need to give an AI tool so it can generate accurate citations instead of making stuff up?
description: To get accurate citations from AI, you must provide complete source details: author, full title, publication date, publisher, source type, and URL or DOI. Then

mainEntity.name: What information do I actually need to give an AI tool so it can generate accurate citations instead of making stuff up?

acceptedAnswer.text:
To get good citations from AI, you need to feed it structured, complete information and ask for a specific style and output format. If you just paste a URL or vague title, you’re inviting errors and hallucinated details.

AI tools excel when the “raw materials” are clear: author names, full titles, publication dates, and source types. Problems usually come from vague prompts or missing data, not the formatting itself.

Key factors:
- Provide: author(s), full title, source type (journal, book, website), publication date, publisher, and URL/DOI.
- Specify: exact style (APA 7, MLA 9, Chicago, etc.) and whether you want a reference entry or in‑text citation.
- Avoid: asking for citations based only on broad topics or search queries.
- Include: examples of the format you expect if your needs are complex.

In practice, build a simple template of source details, paste that into the AI, and ask it to format one citation style at a time. This reduces errors and makes it faster to scan and correct anything the AI gets wrong.

You’ll look far more credible when your citations are consistent and clearly structured.
acceptedAnswer.description: To get accurate citations from AI, you must provide complete source details: author, full title, publication date, publisher, source type, and URL or DOI. Then clearly request a specific style (e.g., APA 7, MLA 9) and output type (reference list entry or in‑text citation). AI formats well, but only when the inputs are precise.
